channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: NASA's Artemis II crew are quite the photographers. See what they've snapped so far
item.description: Many of the photos that have come out of the moon mission so far were taken by crew members. NASA says the crew is getting guidance from scientists on what to capture when they get closer to the moon.
item.content:encoded:
Many of the photos that have come out of the moon mission so far were taken by crew members. NASA says the crew is getting guidance from scientists on what to capture when they get closer to the moon.

(Image credit: Reid Wiseman)
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie

item.title: Trump's ballroom fight sheds new light on an underground White House bunker
item.description: The status of a decades-old bunker beneath the now-demolished East Wing is unclear, but the Trump administration has cited security concerns in its legal filings in favor of continuing construction.
item.content:encoded:
The status of a decades-old bunker beneath the now-demolished East Wing is unclear, but the Trump administration has cited security concerns in its legal filings in favor of continuing construction.

(Image credit: Mandel Ngan)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Seville, Spain's Holy Week blends faith, tradition and spectacle
item.description: Even as religious belief declines in Spain, the processions at Seville's Semana Santa — the Holy Week lead-up to Easter — draw crowds moved by music, tradition and powerful emotion.
item.content:encoded:
Even as religious belief declines in Spain, the processions at Seville's Semana Santa — the Holy Week lead-up to Easter — draw crowds moved by music, tradition and powerful emotion.

(Image credit: Fran Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Alexis Marshall

item.title: Going up! 'Elevator Songs' is a high-concept, emotionally turbulent ride
item.description: On a new album, the experimental vocal group Roomful of Teeth and songwriter Gabriel Kahane take up residence in a multidimensional hotel with a time-traveling elevator and a quirky clientele.
item.content:encoded:
On a new album, the experimental vocal group Roomful of Teeth and songwriter Gabriel Kahane take up residence in a multidimensional hotel with a time-traveling elevator and a quirky clientele.

(Image credit: Anja Schutz)
item.dc:creator: Tom Huizenga

item.title: How Big Thief refreshed its emotional palette on 'Double Infinity'
item.description: The indie rock band's sixth studio album was Big Thief's first as a trio, following the departure of bassist and founding member Max Oleartchik.
item.content:encoded:
The indie rock band's sixth studio album was Big Thief's first as a trio, following the departure of bassist and founding member Max Oleartchik.

(Image credit: Genesis Baez)
item.dc:creator: Raina Douris

item.title: A U.S. jet goes down over Iran, a U.S. official confirms
item.description: A U.S. official said that one crew member had been rescued and U.S. forces continue to search for the second crew member.
item.content:encoded:
A U.S. official said that one crew member had been rescued and U.S. forces continue to search for the second crew member.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: Tom Bowman

item.title: The labor market springs back to life in March as employers add 178,000 jobs
item.description: The U.S. job market perked up last month as employers added 178,000 jobs. The unemployment rate dipped to 4.3%, mainly because the number of people seeking work declined.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. job market perked up last month as employers added 178,000 jobs. The unemployment rate dipped to 4.3%, mainly because the number of people seeking work declined.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Trump budget seeks $1.5 trillion in defense spending alongside domestic program cuts
item.description: In his annual budget, President Trump is asking Congress to boost defense spending to $1.5 trillion, the largest such request in decades.
item.content:encoded:
In his annual budget, President Trump is asking Congress to boost defense spending to $1.5 trillion, the largest such request in decades.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Dark wedding comedy 'The Drama' has a provocative premise — but it never commits
item.description: Early on, Zendaya and Robert Pattinson's new movie The Drama takes a turn. We're talking about it here: spoilers ahead.
item.content:encoded:
Early on, Zendaya and Robert Pattinson's new movie The Drama takes a turn. We're talking about it here: spoilers ahead.

(Image credit: Courtesy of A24)
item.dc:creator: Aisha Harris

item.title: 2 U.S. planes are down and Iran hits Gulf refineries as the war wraps its 5th week
item.description: An F-15 went down in Iran and a second Air Force plane crashed near the Strait of Hormuz as the war capped a week of intensified fighting.
item.content:encoded:
An F-15 went down in Iran and a second Air Force plane crashed near the Strait of Hormuz as the war capped a week of intensified fighting.

(Image credit: Morteza Nikoubazl)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff
